solute.description: This molecule is a substituted phenylalanine derivative consisting of a propanoic acid backbone with an alpha-amino group and a 2,4-dinitrophenyl ring attached to the beta carbon. The aromatic core is specifically functionalized with two strongly electron-withdrawing nitro groups located at the ortho and para positions relative to the alkyl linkage. It is a moderately sized, highly polar molecule characterized by significant hydrogen bonding capacity through its carboxylic acid, primary amine, and four nitro-oxygen atoms. The structure is distinctive for its combination of a potentially zwitterionic amino acid headgroup and a highly electron-deficient aromatic system.

solvent.description: This molecule consists of a short, linear two-carbon saturated chain with no branching or rings. It is characterized by two chlorine atoms covalently bonded to each carbon atom, forming a vicinal dihalide structure. The molecule is small and possesses polar carbon-chlorine bonds, but it lacks any hydrogen bond donating groups.
